packet_structure.name: command_token
packet_structure.constraint: ASCII: 4-byte tokens {USER,PASS,QUIT} for pre-auth, 2-byte tokens {ST,CT,HI,IV} for post-auth. Compared via std::operator== on string (C++), not memcmp.
packet_structure.description: Controls command dispatch in handle_client at 0x1043aa. Matched against literal strings via operator== chain. Unknown commands: pre-auth → '530 Not authenticated', post-auth → '500 Unknown command'.
packet_structure.evidence: branches.log seq_003 field_descriptions[offset:0,size:4,name:command_token] — 4-byte ASCII 'USER' matched; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — chain of operator== calls against 'USER','PASS','QUIT','ST','CT','HI','IV'

packet_structure.name: space_delimiter
packet_structure.description: Separates command from first argument. Consumed by std::operator>> (istream tokenizer).
packet_structure.evidence: branches.log seq_003 field_descriptions[offset:4,size:1,hex:20]; packet_graph.json edges[*].packet_fields[*] consistently show space at offset after command

packet_structure.name: arg1
packet_structure.constraint: Depends on command. USER: username string (config['login']='admin'). PASS: password string (config['password']='secret'). Post-auth: TOKEN-LOCAL-12345 (16 bytes, fixed).
packet_structure.description: First argument to command. For pre-auth USER/PASS: compared against config values. For post-auth commands: must be the auth token 'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345' as string. Token check via operator!= at 0x1044d4 token_check gate.
packet_structure.evidence: branches.log seq_004 field_descriptions[offset:5,size:17,hex:544f4b454e2d4c4f43414c2d3132333435] — 'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345' accepted, server responded 230; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — config['login'] and config['password'] map lookups + operator== comparisons; state.json seeds decompile_str_3='TOKEN-LOCAL-12345'

packet_structure.name: arg2_plus
packet_structure.constraint: Command-specific. ST: two space-separated integers (a b). CT: two space-separated strings (s1 s2). HI: one string (name). IV: two space-separated integers (a b). Parsed via std::istream::operator>>.
packet_structure.description: Command-specific arguments. ST → a+b, CT → s1+s2 (concat), HI → 'Hello ' + name, IV → a*b.
packet_structure.evidence: packet_graph.json edges[003-007] packet_fields show ST a=10 b=20, CT s1=hello s2=world, HI name=World, IV a=7 b=3; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator>> into int a,b or string variables, then calls command_st/ct/hi/iv

packet_structure.name: terminator
packet_structure.constraint: CR (0x0d) or CRLF (0x0d 0x0a). Server accepts CR-only; trim_crlf strips trailing \r and \n.
packet_structure.description: Line terminator. Recv_line reads byte-by-byte until '\n', then trim_crlf removes trailing \r and \n. Both CR and CRLF work.
packet_structure.evidence: branches.log seq_003 field_descriptions[offset:4,hex:20] shows CR-only terminator at offset 10; ghidra_decompile:0x103eff (recv_line) — reads until '\n'; ghidra_decompile:0x103a89 (trim_crlf) — pops back \r and \n characters

value_to_branch.evidence: packet_graph.json edge_001: USER=55534552 routes to 0x1044d4_cmd_dispatch; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator==(command,'USER')
packet_graph.json edge_002: PASS=50415353 routes to 0x1044d4_cmd_dispatch; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator==(command,'PASS')
packet_graph.json edge_007: QUIT=51554954 routes to 0x1044d4; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator==(command,'QUIT') → sends '221 Bye', sets disconnect flag
branches.log seq_004: TOKEN-LOCAL-12345 passed auth, server responded 230; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator!=(token,'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345') → if mismatch sends '403 Bad token'
state.json seeds runtime_cred_admin='admin' from config.ini; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — config.at('login') compared with user via operator==
state.json seeds runtime_cred_secret='secret' from config.ini; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — config.at('password') compared with pass via operator==

sequences.name: USER command — initiate authentication
sequences.description: Send USER <username> after receiving banner. Server responds 331 Password required. Connection stays open.
sequences.evidence: branches.log seq_004: USER admin → 331 Password required; packet_graph.json edge_001; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator==(command,'USER') → sends '331 Password required'

sequences.name: PASS command — complete authentication
sequences.description: After USER, send PASS <password> with config['password']='secret'. On match: auth success, token TOKEN-LOCAL-12345 issued. On mismatch: 530 Authentication failed, connection closed.
sequences.evidence: branches.log seq_005: PASS accepted, 230 TOKEN TOKEN-LOCAL-12345; packet_graph.json edge_002; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — config['login'] match → config['password'] match → bVar3=true (authenticated), sends '230 TOKEN TOKEN-LOCAL-12345'

sequences.name: HI command — greet with name (post-auth)
sequences.description: Post-auth command. First arg must be token TOKEN-LOCAL-12345. Second arg is name to greet. Returns '200 HI Hello <name>'.
sequences.evidence: branches.log seq_006: HI TOKEN-LOCAL-12345 World → 200 HI Hello World; packet_graph.json edge_003; ghidra_decompile:0x1041ba (command_hi) — strcpy + snprintf 'Hello %s'

sequences.name: ST command — sum two integers (post-auth)
sequences.description: Post-auth. ST <token> <a> <b> → '200 ST <a+b>'. Integers parsed via std::istream::operator>>.
sequences.evidence: packet_graph.json edge_004: ST 10 20 → 200 ST 30; ghidra_decompile:0x103fe4 (command_st) — to_string(a+b), prepend '200 ST '

sequences.name: CT command — concatenate two strings (post-auth)
sequences.description: Post-auth. CT <token> <s1> <s2> → '200 CT <s1s2>'. Strings concatenated via std::operator+.
sequences.evidence: packet_graph.json edge_005: CT hello world → 200 CT helloworld; ghidra_decompile:0x10409a (command_ct) — operator+ '200 CT ' + a + b

sequences.name: IV command — multiply two integers (post-auth)
sequences.description: Post-auth. IV <token> <a> <b> → '200 IV product=<a*b>'. Product computed as signed int32 multiplication.
sequences.evidence: packet_graph.json edge_006: IV 7 3 → 200 IV product=21; ghidra_decompile:0x104342 (command_iv) — a*b, to_string, prepend '200 IV product='

sequences.name: QUIT command — graceful disconnect
sequences.description: Send QUIT. Server responds 221 Bye and closes connection. Works both pre-auth and post-auth.
sequences.evidence: packet_graph.json edge_007: QUIT → 221 Bye; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator==(command,'QUIT') → sends '221 Bye', iVar8=1 (exit loop/disconnect)

sequences.name: Error edge cases
sequences.description: Wrong password → 530 + disconnect. Bad token → 403 + stay connected. Unknown pre-auth command → 530 Not authenticated + stay connected.
sequences.evidence: packet_graph.json edges[008-010]: PASS wrongpassword → 530, ST BAD-TOKEN → 403, FOO bar → 530; ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — failure paths with send_line error codes

gates.rule: std::operator== against literal strings: 'USER','PASS','QUIT' (4-byte pre-auth commands), 'ST','CT','HI','IV' (2-byte post-auth commands, only reachable when bVar3==true). Unknown → 530 (pre-auth) or 500 (post-auth).
gates.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — chain of if/else if with operator== comparisons; state.json seeds decompile_str_0 through decompile_str_9

gates.rule: USER <username> must match config['login']. PASS <password> must match config['password']. Both compared via std::operator== on strings. Both must match for bVar3=true (authenticated flag). Config sourced from config.ini with keys 'login'='admin' and 'password'='secret'.
gates.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — config.at('login') compared to user; config.at('password') compared to pass; state.json seeds runtime_cred_admin, runtime_cred_secret from config.ini

gates.rule: Post-auth commands require first argument to be exactly 'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345' (16 bytes). Compared via std::operator!=. Bad token → '403 Bad token', iVar8=0 (stay connected). Good token → proceed to command handler.
gates.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator!=(token,'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345'); branches.log seq_004: TOKEN-LOCAL-12345 accepted; state.json seeds decompile_str_3

gates.rule: iVar8=0 → continue loop (USER, auth success, unknown pre-auth, bad token). iVar8=1 → exit/disconnect (QUIT, bad password, bad auth). iVar8=2 → continue loop (post-auth command success, unknown post-auth). Loop condition: while (iVar8==0 || iVar8==2).
gates.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — final if/else chain and do-while condition

field_influence_map.description: Primary dispatch field. Pre-auth: 4-byte tokens USER, PASS, QUIT compared via operator== chain. USER → username path (331 response). PASS → password/auth path. QUIT → disconnect (221 Bye). Post-auth (after bVar3=true): 2-byte tokens ST, CT, HI, IV compared similarly. ST → integer sum. CT → string concat. HI → greet with strcpy. IV → integer product with memset. Any unknown token: 530 Not authenticated (pre-auth) or 500 Unknown command (post-auth).
field_influence_map.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa full function; packet_graph.json field_influence_map command; branches.log seq_003-006 field_descriptions

field_influence_map.description: Username for USER command. Stored in 'user' string variable. Later compared against config['login'] value during PASS handling (not during USER handling). Must equal 'admin' for authentication to succeed. If mismatched during PASS phase, auth fails.
field_influence_map.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator>> into 'user', later config.at('login') comparison; state.json seeds runtime_cred_admin='admin'

field_influence_map.description: Password for PASS command. Compared against config['password']='secret' via operator==. Match → bVar3=true, sends '230 TOKEN TOKEN-LOCAL-12345', loop continues. Mismatch → '530 Authentication failed', iVar8=1, connection closed.
field_influence_map.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — config.at('password') comparison; state.json seeds runtime_cred_secret='secret'

field_influence_map.description: Authentication token. Required as first argument to all post-auth commands (ST/CT/HI/IV). Must be exactly 'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345' (16 ASCII bytes). Compared via operator!=. Wrong token → '403 Bad token', loop continues (no disconnect). Correct token → command-specific handler invoked.
field_influence_map.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — operator!=(token,'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345'); branches.log seq_004; state.json seeds decompile_str_3

field_influence_map.description: Command-specific operands. ST: two integers a,b (sum → '200 ST <a+b>'). CT: two strings s1,s2 (concat → '200 CT <s1s2>'). HI: one string name (greet → '200 HI Hello <name>', uses strcpy into 24-byte buffer — VULNERABLE). IV: two integers a,b (product → '200 IV product=<a*b>', uses memset with product as length — potential overflow). All parsed via std::istream::operator>>.

risk_hints.description: CRITICAL Stack buffer overflow in command_hi at 0x1041ba. User-controlled name string (from istream::operator>>, no length limit) is copied via strcpy into a 24-byte stack buffer (name_buffer[24]) with NO bounds check. Then snprintf(result, 0x28, 'Hello %s', name_buffer) formats result, but the overflow already occurred in strcpy. Stack canary present (__stack_chk_fail at 0x103790) provides partial mitigation. Reachable by: (1) authenticate with USER admin + PASS secret → get token, (2) send 'HI TOKEN-LOCAL-12345 <payload>' where payload exceeds 23 bytes to overwrite return address. SEVERITY: HIGH — direct RIP control possible if canary bypassed via info leak.
risk_hints.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1041ba — 'char name_buffer [24]; strcpy(name_buffer, __src);' where __src is c_str() of user-supplied name; no length validation between operator>> and strcpy

risk_hints.description: Resource exhaustion — memory bomb in recv_line at 0x103eff. Reads one byte at a time via recv(fd, &ch, 1, 0) into a C++ std::string via push_back with NO maximum line length. An attacker can send a continuous stream of bytes without '\n' to force unbounded string growth. Each push_back may trigger reallocation (×2 growth), leading to memory exhaustion and eventual OOM kill of the server. Impact: DoS via single connection. SEVERITY: MEDIUM — no code execution, but reliable DoS.
risk_hints.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x103eff — 'do { recv(fd,&ch,1,0); push_back(); } while (ch != '\n');' — no length check, no timeout, no max iterations

risk_hints.description: Signed integer overflow in command_iv at 0x104342. Product a*b computed as signed int32 multiplication (__val = a * b). If product overflows to a value in [0, 63], the memset path executes with the overflowed value as length. If product is negative (overflow), check '-1 < __val' fails and memset is skipped (safe). Practical exploitation requires finding a*b mod 2^32 in [0,63] with large operands, which is mathematically constrained but should not be ruled out. The scratch buffer is 64 bytes; any __val < 64 writes safely. Additionally, if product is in [0,63], it fills scratch with 'A's and null-terminates — no immediate overflow but unexpected behavior. SEVERITY: LOW — difficult to exploit meaningfully.
risk_hints.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x104342 — 'int __val = a * b; if ((-1 < __val) && (__val < 0x40)) { memset(scratch,0x41,(long)__val); scratch[__val] = '\0'; }' — signed 32-bit multiply with overflow-sensitive gate

risk_hints.description: NOTE: Not a confirmed UaF, but the Ghidra decompile shows complex C++ string lifecycle with manual destructor calls interleaved with allocator destructors. String temporaries (local_1e8, b_1) are destroyed at various points during command handling. The decompiler shows this pattern: basic_string constructor → send_line → manual string destructor → allocator destructor. If send_line throws or the control flow is interrupted at the wrong point, C++ exceptions may leave strings in inconsistent state. However, std::string uses RAII normally; the manual destructor pattern here is likely Ghidra decompilation artifact from inlined code. SEVERITY: INFO — not confirmed, but pattern warrants fuzzing attention.
risk_hints.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1043aa — manual sequence of basic_string constructor, send_line, ~string, ~__new_allocator interleaved with complex if/else branches

risk_hints.description: Potential format string in command_hi at 0x1041ba. snprintf(result, 0x28, 'Hello %s', name_buffer) uses a proper format specifier with user data as %s argument — this is SAFE as written. Format string is hardcoded 'Hello %s', not attacker-controlled. However, if the snprintf call is incorrectly decompiled (e.g., if the format string actually includes user data in another code path), this could be a vector. Based on current decompile: NOT exploitable, format string is constant. SEVERITY: INFO — constant format string, likely safe.
risk_hints.evidence: ghidra_decompile:0x1041ba — 'snprintf(result,0x28,"Hello %s",name_buffer,...)' — format string is hardcoded literal

unreached_branches: 0x1044d4 (branch hooks at 0, but logic validated via decompile and runtime response observation)
graph_summary.decision_tree:
ROOT(connect:2121)
  → [banner] send_line('220 SimpleTCP ready')
  → recv_line() loop:
    ├─ command=='USER'
    │   → read username
    │   → send_line('331 Password required')
    │   → continue loop
    ├─ command=='PASS'
    │   → read password
    │   → config['login'] == stored username?
    │       ├─ YES → config['password'] == password?
    │       │   ├─ YES → bVar3=true, send_line('230 TOKEN TOKEN-LOCAL-12345'), continue loop (now authenticated)
    │       │   └─ NO  → send_line('530 Authentication failed'), disconnect
    │       └─ NO  → send_line('530 Authentication failed'), disconnect
    ├─ command=='QUIT'
    │   → send_line('221 Bye'), disconnect
    ├─ [AUTHENTICATED] bVar3==true
    │   → read token
    │   ├─ token != 'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345'
    │   │   → send_line('403 Bad token'), continue loop
    │   └─ token == 'TOKEN-LOCAL-12345'
    │       ├─ command=='ST' → read a,b → command_st(a,b) → send_line('200 ST <sum>')
    │       ├─ command=='CT' → read s1,s2 → command_ct(s1,s2) → send_line('200 CT <concat>')
    │       ├─ command=='HI' → read name → command_hi(name) [strcpy vuln!] → send_line('200 HI Hello <name>')
    │       ├─ command=='IV' → read a,b → command_iv(a,b) [int overflow] → send_line('200 IV product=<product>')
    │       └─ else → send_line('500 Unknown command'), continue loop
    └─ [UNAUTHENTICATED] bVar3==false
        └─ send_line('530 Not authenticated'), continue loop

coverage.note: All 200+ Ghidra branch hooks returned 0 hit counts across 8 probes despite correct rebase verification and live protocol processing. Binary uses C++ std::operator== string comparisons (not memcmp/strcmp) for command dispatch and std::istringstream for tokenization — these are likely compiled to inline/invariant code paths that don't trigger the static branch addresses. Protocol logic was fully validated through: (a) Ghidra decompile of handle_client (0x1043aa, 2637 bytes), (b) runtime packet exchange observed via Tier 2 recv_libc/send_libc hooks, (c) 8 successful sequences with expected responses. All seeds from decompile confirmed operational.
